pr.title: lb: implement lb_80019628; improve four lbbgflash functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Implemented a C body for lb_80019628 in lb_0192.c and made targeted matching improvements to four lbbgflash.c functions. The new lb_80019628 handles pad sampling period maintenance using lb_804329F0 state, OS_TIMER_CLOCK-derived period caps, PADSetSamplingRate, and periodic OSAlarm rescheduling through fn_800195FC. The lbbgflash changes are mostly compiler-shaping edits: branch-local target/pointer locals for RGBA channel saturation, reordered squared-component math for scheduling, a volatile Quaternion pointer to preserve inline assert behavior, and an explicit if/else for parent selection. No function reached 100%, but fuzzy match scores improved across five functions and build verification passed with GALE01 main.dol OK.
what_changed: Added lb_80019628 in src/melee/lb/lb_0192.c, replacing an asm-only fallback with a C implementation that snapshots lb_804329F0.x38, updates lb_804329F0.x0[0].x0, resets x0[0].x8 when it exceeds the new limit, derives a minimum period, updates PADSetSamplingRate, and recreates/reschedules the periodic OSAlarm.
In lb_80019628, the period is bounded against OS_TIMER_CLOCK, lb_804329F0.x0[0].x0, lb_804329F0.x0[1].x0, and a 1/60-second fps_period computed as 0.016666668f * OS_TIMER_CLOCK.
In lb_80019628, sampling rate is computed as period divided by OS_TIMER_CLOCK / 1000, capped at 11, and only applied when lb_804329F0.x4 differs.
In fn_8001FC08, each RGBA saturation block now declares branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals, then updates *cur toward the byte target depending on whether x20[i] is positive or non-positive.

In lbBgFlash_80020E38, squared component locals and the zero-vector sum were reordered from dz2/dx2/dy2 and dz2 + dy2 + dx2 to dx2/dy2/dz2 and dx2 + dy2 + dz2 to better match original floating-point scheduling.

In fn_8002113C, uses Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr = &rot in the non-quaternion path and passes rot_ptr through Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation, HSD_MkRotationMtx, HSD_QuatLib_8037EB28, and FakeHSD_JObjSetRotation.
In fn_80020AEC, expanded a ternary parent assignment into an explicit if/else so the compiler emits branch ordering closer to the original.

Reported fuzzy improvements: lb_80019628 from 0% source body to 82.58%, fn_8001FC08 from 89.55% to 91.77%, fn_8002113C from 93.17% to 94.72%, fn_80020AEC from 88.27% to 88.77%, and lbBgFlash_80020E38 from 98.46% to 98.52%.
Verification reported: python configure.py && ninja passed, build/GALE01/main.dol OK, with sibling functions in both translation units unchanged.
smart_moves: Used early returns in lb_80019628 when the requested value or resulting period was unchanged, avoiding unnecessary PAD and alarm work while matching observed control flow.

Preserved the original OSAlarm lifecycle pattern by cancelling an existing alarm when x48 is nonzero, creating a fresh alarm, then setting a periodic alarm with fn_800195FC as OSAlarmHandler and marking x48 active.

Matched compiler register allocation in fn_8001FC08 by introducing branch-local target and cur locals, keeping byte targets and x10 pointers live in the desired registers across rising/falling saturation logic.
Used source-level expression ordering, not semantic changes, to influence MWCC scheduling in lbBgFlash_80020E38.
Used a volatile pointer to prevent MWCC from proving &rot non-null and eliding inline-substituted asserts in Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation, preserving code shape at the referenced jobj.h assert sites.
Changed a compact ternary into explicit control flow in fn_80020AEC to match the original bne / b / li branch sequence.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC matching, semantically equivalent C can differ substantially in register allocation and instruction scheduling; branch-local temporaries can be a deliberate matching tool.

When an inline function contains asserts, the compiler may optimize them away if it can prove pointer arguments are non-null. Routing the address through a volatile pointer can preserve the original assert call structure.
Floating-point local declaration order and addition order can affect lfs/fmuls/fadds scheduling; preserve the original computation order even when addition is mathematically commutative.
Ternary expressions may compile to different branch ordering than explicit if/else; use explicit control flow when the original has a recognizable branch layout.
Implementing an asm-only function can still be valuable without a 100% match when it improves fuzzy score and documents behavior while leaving sibling functions unchanged.
For OS timer and PAD maintenance code, guard hardware/API calls with state comparisons so the C reflects both behavior and original early-return shape.
naming_conventions: Existing unknown/global names were preserved: lb_804329F0, lb_80019628, fn_800195FC, fn_8001FC08, fn_8002113C, fn_80020AEC, lbBgFlash_80020E38.
Temporary names in the new implementation describe inferred roles without over-renaming unknown fields: alarm, period, new_val, fps_period, rate.
Matching-oriented locals in lbbgflash use concise role names target and cur for channel byte target and current float pointer.
Quaternion volatile workaround uses rot_ptr, making the indirection explicit while keeping existing rot/rot2 naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Branch-local u8 target and f32* cur locals in fn_8001FC08 were used to keep the target color byte and x10[i] pointer in the desired registers across rising/falling channel saturation blocks.
Reordered dx2, dy2, dz2 declarations and changed the zero-length sum to dx2 + dy2 + dz2 to match original floating-point pipeline scheduling.
Introduced Quaternion* volatile rot_ptr in fn_8002113C to block non-null proof optimization and retain Fake_HSD_JObjGetRotation inline assert codegen.
Expanded parent = jobj ? jobj->parent : NULL into explicit if/else in fn_80020AEC to match original branch sequencing.

Used explicit casts around OS_TIMER_CLOCK and floating-point period math in lb_80019628, including (OSTime) (f64) (u32) OS_TIMER_CLOCK and 0.016666668f * (f32) (u32) OS_TIMER_CLOCK, likely to match original conversion behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.

The PR summary itself notes no 100% matches and frames the changes as five improvements toward the original, which is important evidence that these were incremental matching gains rather than completed decompilation for all touched functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete lb_80019628 to 100% or further refine lb_0192.c pad sampling/alarm behavior.
Search for other uses of Quaternion* volatile or volatile pointer indirection to preserve inline asserts in HSD JObj code.
Search for additional lbbgflash.c matching PRs touching fn_8001FC08, lbBgFlash_80020E38, fn_8002113C, or fn_80020AEC.
Compare other PADSetSamplingRate and OSSetPeriodicAlarm implementations for consistent OS_TIMER_CLOCK conversion and rate cap handling.
Look for matching notes where branch-local pointer/target locals are used to force MWCC register allocation.
